div.head: The Consul General at Shanghai (Gauss) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 370. Reference my 347, March 2, 7 p.m. Japanese Consulate General has now replied to radio companies that it is unable to accept their proposals, adding that while obstacles continue to exist to use of the Chinese Government Radio Administration equipment in the French concession they are doing their utmost to restore radio communications and feel confident that the Shanghai station under Japanese control will succeed in rendering satisfactory service at no distant date.
